Question Text: Is there a formally approved system of record for leads, opportunities, customers, invoices, and renewal data, or do different teams rely on different sources?
Evidence Type: System inventory; source-owner interviews; reporting source map

Question Text: Are spreadsheets used for analysis and presentation only, rather than to create, update, or override pipeline, billing, or customer-status data outside the controlled system?
Evidence Type: Spreadsheet inventory; version history; reporting workflow walkthrough

Question Text: Are CRM, finance, service, and reporting fields aligned so that account, deal, invoice, and customer records can be reconciled without manual remapping?
Evidence Type: Field dictionary; object mapping; integration or export review

Question Text: Can each critical KPI be traced to a defined source object, exact field set, owner, and refresh method?
Evidence Type: KPI list; metric dictionary; dashboard logic review

Question Text: Are required fields enforced at the stage where work occurs, including owner, status, amount, dates, source, and next-step data?
Evidence Type: Field configuration review; required-field rules; record sample test

Question Text: Are stage changes, close dates, invoice dates, renewal dates, and ownership updates made within an agreed timeframe after the real-world event occurs?
Evidence Type: Timestamp audit; workflow SLA review; record-history sample

Question Text: Do teams use the same data-entry rules for naming, stage/status selection, reason codes, and record completion across the revenue workflow?
Evidence Type: User guide / SOP review; cross-team record sample; manager interviews

Question Text: Can reporting run without last-minute manual cleanup because records are maintained correctly throughout the month?
Evidence Type: Month-end reporting walkthrough; cleanup log; reporting owner interviews

Question Text: Are duplicate accounts, contacts, opportunities, invoices, and products actively prevented or resolved before they distort counts and reporting?
Evidence Type: Duplicate scan; merge rules; sample record review

Question Text: Is the core data needed for funnel, forecast, billing, cash-collection, and retention reporting complete enough to avoid manual patching?
Evidence Type: Required-field completeness test; KPI input review; report rebuild sample

Question Text: Are records current and aligned to the latest business event, rather than sitting in stale stages or outdated statuses?
Evidence Type: Record aging review; stage-status audit; event-to-system comparison

Question Text: Are categories such as lead source, loss reason, product/service, region, invoice status, and customer type used consistently enough for reliable roll-up analysis?
Evidence Type: Taxonomy review; picklist audit; grouped report test

Question Text: Can the data show the full path from lead creation through qualification, proposal, close, invoicing, payment, onboarding, and renewal without major blind spots?
Evidence Type: Lifecycle map; end-to-end record trace; management report review

Question Text: Are cross-functional handoffs visible in the data, with timestamps, owners, and status markers when work moves between marketing, sales, operations, finance, and customer teams?
Evidence Type: Handoff field review; timestamp test; workflow interviews

Question Text: Can the business measure stage aging, rework, backlog, and drop-off across its critical revenue and service workflows?
Evidence Type: Aging reports; queue review; stage-conversion analysis

Question Text: Do system fields and statuses reflect how work actually happens, including exceptions and rework, rather than an idealised process that users bypass?
Evidence Type: Process walkthrough; status-field audit; exception case review

Question Text: Are KPIs defined with clear numerators, denominators, inclusion/exclusion rules, source fields, refresh cadence, and business intent?
Evidence Type: Metric definitions; formula review; source-field mapping

Question Text: Do dashboards, spreadsheet models, and management packs use the same KPI formulas and period logic?
Evidence Type: Cross-report formula test; dashboard comparison; period logic review

Question Text: Are targets, thresholds, and benchmark bands defined for the KPIs leadership uses to run the business?
Evidence Type: Target-setting review; leadership packs; KPI threshold register

Question Text: Is each KPI assigned to an owner responsible for maintaining the definition, validating the result, and driving action from it?
Evidence Type: Ownership matrix; KPI governance review; management interviews

Question Text: Do dashboards and reporting packs surface the decisions, exceptions, and risks leaders actually need to manage, rather than just listing raw figures?
Evidence Type: Dashboard walkthrough; management pack review; leadership interviews

Question Text: Are reports refreshed from controlled source data with minimal manual manipulation, version confusion, or offline adjustments?
Evidence Type: Refresh workflow review; version-control check; report preparation sample

Question Text: Can leadership quickly identify pipeline slippage, stale records, billing backlog, renewal risk, and data-quality exceptions from current reporting?
Evidence Type: Exception report review; dashboard test; leadership meeting pack

Question Text: Are teams using one published management pack or dashboard logic, rather than parallel reports that say different things?
Evidence Type: Report inventory; source comparison; stakeholder interviews

Question Text: Are named owners accountable for source data quality, KPI definitions, dashboard logic, and report sign-off?
Evidence Type: RACI / ownership map; governance documents; manager interviews

Question Text: Is there a formal cadence for reviewing data quality, KPI accuracy, reporting issues, and unresolved exceptions?
Evidence Type: Meeting cadence review; governance calendar; issue log

Question Text: Are SOPs, field rules, change controls, and approval steps documented for data capture, KPI changes, and reporting updates?
Evidence Type: SOP review; change request sample; control documentation

Question Text: Are data issues logged, prioritised, root-caused, and resolved through a controlled process rather than informal fixes?
Evidence Type: Issue log; remediation tracker; root-cause review
